# TaskFlow Doc Drift Report

**Date:** 2026-04-11
**Generated by:** TaskFlow feature audit — Phase 5 (Task 19)
**Branch:** `audit/taskflow-2026-04-11`

## Purpose

The `.claude/skills/add-taskflow/templates/CLAUDE.md.template` and
`docs/taskflow-*.md` files are the canonical sources. Each provisioned
group retains a frozen snapshot under `groups/<name>/` that was copied
at provisioning time. This report measures how far each group has
drifted from the canonical source so future work can decide whether to
re-sync.

This is a **read-only report**. Group copies are never edited by this
audit. The purpose is to scope a separate reconciliation task later if
desired.

## Methodology

### CLAUDE.md template drift

For each `groups/<name>/CLAUDE.md`, compare it against
`.claude/skills/add-taskflow/templates/CLAUDE.md.template` via a
line-level `diff` of both sorted:

- **Lines added** — lines present in the group file but not in the
  canonical template.
- **Lines removed** — lines present in the canonical template but not
  in the group file.
- **Total group lines** — current line count of the group file.

Sorting is intentional: it measures line-identity drift, not
line-order drift. A reordering of otherwise identical lines reports
`added=0 removed=0`.

### Per-doc drift (`taskflow-user-manual.md`, `taskflow-quick-start.md`)

For each group snapshot, count the number of `+` and `-` lines in a
unified diff against the canonical source (excluding diff
metadata). This is the raw churn count — additions and deletions
combined.

Groups that do not carry a local copy of a canonical doc are omitted
from that section's table (they inherit the canonical source via the
mount, and there is nothing to drift).

## CLAUDE.md template drift

| Group | Lines added (group has, template lacks) | Lines removed (template has, group lacks) | Total group lines |
|---|---|---|---|
| ci-seci-taskflow | 44 | 67 | 1092 |
| e2e-taskflow | 44 | 67 | 1092 |
| eurotrip | 218 | 1047 | 286 |
| global | 72 | 1072 | 115 |
| laizys-taskflow | 44 | 67 | 1092 |
| main | 212 | 1018 | 309 |
| sec-secti | 45 | 67 | 1093 |
| seci-taskflow | 44 | 67 | 1092 |
| secti-taskflow | 44 | 67 | 1092 |
| setec-secti-taskflow | 44 | 67 | 1092 |
| tec-taskflow | 44 | 67 | 1092 |
| test-taskflow | 44 | 67 | 1092 |
| thiago-taskflow | 44 | 67 | 1092 |
| ux-setd-secti-taskflow | 44 | 67 | 1092 |
| whatsapp_main | 27 | 1094 | 48 |

**Highest drift (sum of added + removed):**

1. `eurotrip` — 1265 lines of churn (218 added, 1047 removed; total 286)
2. `main` — 1230 lines of churn (212 added, 1018 removed; total 309)
3. `whatsapp_main` — 1121 lines of churn (27 added, 1094 removed; total 48)

**Interpretation:**

- The three most-drifted groups (`eurotrip`, `main`, `whatsapp_main`)
  are **not TaskFlow groups**. Their line counts (286, 309, 48) are
  far below the ~1092 baseline of any TaskFlow group. They carry
  completely different CLAUDE.md content purpose-built for travel,
  main-assistant, and WhatsApp-auth workflows respectively. Their
  "drift" is really "they were never derived from the TaskFlow
  template" — this is expected, not a reconciliation target.
- The 14 TaskFlow groups cluster tightly around `added=44 removed=67
  total~1092`, with `sec-secti` off by one (+1 added). This shows
  they were all provisioned from the same template snapshot and
  evolved identically under canonical template updates. The constant
  `67` removed lines likely reflects post-provisioning content the
  template has since gained (or, conversely, provisioned boilerplate
  the template no longer carries); the constant `44` added lines
  likely reflects per-group customization (board name, admin name,
  group JID, assistant name) and local post-provisioning additions
  that never propagated back to the template.
- `sec-secti` being off by one (`added=45` vs. the 44 baseline)
  indicates one extra local customization — likely a single
  hand-edit. Harmless but worth noting.

## User manual drift

| Group | Diff lines vs canonical |
|---|---|
| sec-secti | 435 |
| seci-taskflow | 435 |
| secti-taskflow | 435 |
| tec-taskflow | 435 |

**Highest drift:** all four groups that carry a local
`taskflow-user-manual.md` are tied at 435 diff lines.

**Interpretation:**

- Only 4 of the 15 groups carry a local user-manual snapshot. The
  other 11 either do not use TaskFlow or mount the canonical doc at
  runtime without a frozen copy.
- The identical `435` counts across all four strongly suggest they
  were all snapshotted at the **same** pre-Phase-4 point in time and
  none of them received the Phase 4 Task 12 canonical sync
  commits. The Phase 4 churn on `docs/taskflow-user-manual.md` is
  the dominant component of this drift.

## Quick-start drift

| Group | Diff lines vs canonical |
|---|---|
| sec-secti | 94 |
| seci-taskflow | 94 |
| secti-taskflow | 94 |
| tec-taskflow | 94 |

**Highest drift:** all four groups that carry a local
`taskflow-quick-start.md` are tied at 94 diff lines.

**Interpretation:**

- Same 4-group population as the user-manual.
- The identical `94` count is strong evidence of the same shared
  snapshot origin. Quick-start drift is smaller because the quick-
  start underwent less Phase 4 churn than the user-manual.

## Recommendation

This report does **not** drive any edits in the current audit. Its
purpose is to quantify the gap so a later reconciliation task can be
scoped.

If a reconciliation is undertaken:

1. **TaskFlow groups only.** Leave `eurotrip`, `main`, and
   `whatsapp_main` out of scope — they are not TaskFlow groups.
2. **User-manual first.** The `435` lines of drift represents Phase 4
   doc improvements that TaskFlow users on the 4 drifted boards are
   not currently seeing.
3. **Then quick-start.** Smaller payoff, same mechanical approach.
4. **CLAUDE.md template last, or never.** The 44/67 drift on
   TaskFlow groups is structural (per-group customization vs.
   template evolution). A pure re-sync would clobber local
   customizations; a three-way merge is needed. Out of scope for a
   simple reconciliation pass.
5. **Investigate `sec-secti`'s extra line.** One-off hand edit; worth
   capturing the intent before any re-sync to avoid losing it.

The 11 TaskFlow groups without local doc snapshots are already
reading the canonical mount; no action needed there.
